metadata.notes:
agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready

## Primitive

Model metadata reporting: pi's JSONL rollout records the active model name on every assistant message in a field the parser extracts, so the dashboard shows the launch model rather than 'unknown'.

## agent_supports — yes

pi boots on a concrete model and writes that model into the rollout on every assistant turn. There is no way to run a turn without a model, so a single basic turn is enough to exercise this scenario — the model name is present from turn 1.

## irrlicht_observes — yes

The pi parser reads the model from two places, both normalized through tailer.NormalizeModelName (lowercases, strips provider prefixes): assistant messages carry it at message.model (parser.go L104 → ev.ModelName), and model_change events carry it at modelId (parser.go L40-42). ev.ModelName flows into SessionMetrics.ModelName, populated from the first assistant turn onward. extractPiContribution (parser.go L148) also tags the per-turn contribution with the same model name.

## Observability caveat — where the assertion lives

The expected.jsonl validator DSL (validate/expected.go Phase struct: phase / expected_state / kind / max_delay_ms / duration_at_least_ms / same_session_as / new_session / invariants) operates over the lifecycle events.jsonl stream, which carries NO model_name field — so expected.jsonl cannot directly assert the model name. The model-identification proof is the metrics-level summary.model_name in the .replay.json.golden snapshot (non-empty / not 'unknown'), validated by replay-fixtures, plus the pi parser unit tests on message.model extraction. expected.jsonl therefore asserts only the basic-turn lifecycle skeleton (session born ready → working → ready, no waiting, a single process_exited); the model presence is the golden's / parser-test's job. Per issue #440 the concrete model_name string is environment-dependent (whatever model the account boots on), so the assertion is 'a model_name IS present', not a hardcoded value.

## Conclusion

applicable: yes. A thin cell — the basic-turn arc plus the (metrics-level) assertion that a model_name was extracted from message.model.
caveats: ContextWindow may briefly read 0 between session detection and the first assistant event; assert model/context only after wait_turn, not at presession_created.

The concrete model_name is environment-dependent (pi's boot default); per issue #440 the spec/golden assertion is that a model_name is present (non-empty, not 'unknown'), not a specific hardcoded string, so the cell stays deterministic and worktree-portable.

The model name lives at metrics level (SessionMetrics.ModelName), not in the events.jsonl lifecycle stream the expected.jsonl DSL operates over — so the DSL spec asserts only the lifecycle arc; the model claim is covered by parser unit tests + the replay golden.

sources.note: model_change.modelId and assistant message.model → ev.ModelName via tailer.NormalizeModelName
extractPiContribution tags the per-turn contribution with the same model name
Phase struct has no model field — DSL asserts state/kind on events.jsonl, not model_name
replay-summary model_name is environment-dependent — assert presence, not a hardcoded string

preconditions: pi CLI on PATH and authenticated (pi runs against its configured model out-of-band)

tmux available — the interactive driver runs pi in a tmux REPL so it can send one prompt to a long-lived session and tear it down cleanly (per concern C6: headless pi --print exits too fast and drifts)
irrlichd is recording — either run-cell.sh in isolated mode, or --attach against a running daemon started with --record

The concrete model pi boots on is environment-dependent. Per issue #440 the assertion is that a model_name is PRESENT (non-empty, not 'unknown'), not a specific hardcoded string — do not assert a particular model value.
setup: The interactive driver (drive-pi-interactive.sh) launches pi in a fresh per-run cwd inside a tmux session, waits for the REPL prompt, then sends the single prompt
pi writes one JSONL rollout file per session under ~/.pi/agent/sessions/<cwd-slug>/ on launch (FilesUnderRoot, non-lazy); the driver resolves that file and extracts the UUID from line 1

No special model flag — pi boots on its configured model and writes that model into each assistant message.model from turn 1; the scenario is about the daemon extracting that name, not about which model produced the tokens
The send bumps the expected-turn counter; wait_turn blocks until the rollout shows a completed assistant turn, so the trailing sleep never races a still-running turn
ModelName is checked AFTER turn_end — ContextWindow may briefly read 0 between session detection and the first assistant event (per assessment caveat)
After wait_turn + trailing sleep the driver kills the tmux session once — pi exits and the daemon emits a single process_exited for the rollout session

script.text: Reply with exactly the word: ok

verify: events.jsonl contains a transcript_new for the rollout session born ready within ~2s of REPL launch (a transient proc-<PID> pre-session may also appear; the turn and pid_discovered land on the UUID-keyed rollout session)
events.jsonl contains a pid_discovered event bound to the rollout session_id
events.jsonl shows one working→ready arc on the rollout session_id (the single assistant turn); the interactive REPL returns to ready, unlike headless pi --print which exits

the .replay.json.golden carries a non-empty summary.model_name (NOT 'unknown') — the pi parser pulled it from message.model and the tailer applied it. The concrete value is environment-dependent (issue #440); assert presence, not a hardcoded string
the rollout session's final live state is ready before the driver tears the REPL down
events.jsonl contains exactly one process_exited on the rollout session at REPL teardown
no state_transition to waiting appears for the rollout session at any point
recipe.notes: Thin/near-trivial cell: structurally identical to pi/basic-turn (one send + wait_turn + trailing sleep) — the only extra intent over basic-turn is that the daemon extracted a model_name. WHY interactive (not headless pi --print): per concern C6 the headless single-shot driver exits too fast and drifts; the pi interactive driver (drive-pi-interactive.sh) keeps the rollout REPL alive via send+wait_turn and kills the tmux session once at the end. The model lives on each assistant message.model (parser.go L104) — and on model_change events at modelId (L41) — which the pi parser pulls through tailer.NormalizeModelName into ev.ModelName → SessionMetrics.ModelName from turn 1. The model assertion is NOT expressible in the events.jsonl phase DSL (no model field in the validator Phase struct), so expected.jsonl covers only the basic-turn lifecycle skeleton and the model presence is asserted at summary.model_name in the .replay.json.golden (which replay-fixtures.sh diffs) plus the pi parser unit tests. C7/SESSION-IDENTITY: the live REPL emits a transient proc-<PID> pre-session born ready at launch; session_birth (which only asserts 'a session appears in ready within 2s') legitimately matches it. turn_start anchors to recording start with NO session pin so the matcher picks the FIRST working in the whole recording (necessarily the rollout session — the pre-session never goes working); turn_end, pid_bind, and teardown then pin to turn_start's session via same_session_as. What differs between runs: the concrete model_name (boot default, env-dependent per #440), token counts/cost, rollout UUID/PID/timestamps, assistant wording (pinned with 'Reply with exactly the word: ok'). What must NOT differ: one working→ready arc on one rollout session, a non-empty model_name in the golden, and a single process_exited at teardown.
